questions.prompt: What's automatic in Cloudflare DDoS protection vs. what you tune?

choices.text: L3/L4 (network volumetric) protection is automatic, always on, free, with hundreds of Tbps absorbed capacity across PoPs. L7 (HTTP) protection has automatic signature detection but app-specific patterns need WAF rules + rate limiting + bot management. The most expensive attack class (volumetric) is handled for you; the targeted ones you wire up.

questions.explanation: Volumetric DDoS is structurally absorbed by Cloudflare's network — anycast distribution + huge capacity means most volumetric attacks never reach origin. Targeted L7 attacks specific to your app need explicit WAF + rate limit + bot tuning.

questions.prompt: Your password-reset endpoint is being abused for account enumeration — steady probing across many IPs, low per-IP rate. Right mitigation?

choices.text: Trust Cloudflare's automatic DDoS to handle it.

Rate limit on the specific endpoint (e.g. 5 requests per IP per minute on /password-reset), add bot management challenges, possibly add CAPTCHA. This is app-specific abuse — not volumetric — so automatic DDoS detection won't catch it. WAF rules + rate limits + bot management are the right layer.

questions.explanation: Application-logic abuse needs application-aware defenses. Rate limiting on the specific endpoint, bot management for automated traffic, and CAPTCHA for high-risk paths. Cloudflare's WAF + rate limit + bot tools wire to this case.
